title: Revamp Accounting System with Double-Entry Bookkeeping & Modern UX
description:
## Problem Statement

Current accounting module (`/en/accounting`) has fundamental design issues:

1. **Incorrect Accounting Method**: Simplified single-entry table view instead of proper double-entry bookkeeping
2. **No Chart of Accounts**: Can't track debits/credits properly for assets, liabilities, equity, revenue, expenses
3. **Poor UX**: Basic table view, not intuitive for non-accountants
4. **Missing AR Reconciliation Integration**: E-commerce platform fees (Shopee/Lazada) not properly tracked when closing AR periods
5. **Non-Standard Workflows**: Users manually change status instead of system following GAAP/IFRS/MAS-8 accounting standards

## Scope

### Phase 1: Core Accounting System (Double-Entry)

**A. Chart of Accounts**
- Define standard chart of accounts (GAAP/IFRS compliant)
- Allow business to customize chart of accounts
- Support account hierarchy (parent-child accounts)

**B. Double-Entry Journal Entries**
- Every transaction creates balanced journal entry (debit = credit)
- Validation: Every journal entry must balance
- Support reversing entries

**C. Automated Journal Entry Creation**
- Sales invoice sent → Dr. AR, Cr. Revenue
- AR recon close period → Dr. Cash, Dr. Platform Fees, Cr. AR
- Expense approved → Dr. Expense, Cr. AP

**D. Financial Statements (Auto-Generated)**
- Profit & Loss Statement
- Balance Sheet
- Cash Flow Statement
- Trial Balance

### Phase 2: AR Reconciliation Integration

- Close period creates proper entries for platform fees and bank deposits
- Update invoice status to "paid"
- Link journal entries to AR reconciliation record

### Phase 3: Modern UX Design

**A. Dashboard View (Replacing Table)**
- Card-based layout showing key metrics
- Visual charts (revenue vs expenses, expense breakdown, cash flow)
- Quick actions

**B. Transaction List (Enhanced)**
- Group transactions by journal entry
- Expandable rows to see debit/credit details
- Enhanced filtering and search

**C. Simplified Language**
- User-facing labels ("Money In" instead of "Debit Cash")
- Toggle "Accountant Mode" for debit/credit terminology

## Design Inspiration

- QuickBooks Online: Dashboard with metrics, simplified language
- Xero: Clean card-based layout, visual charts
- Wave Accounting: User-friendly for non-accountants
- FreshBooks: Beautiful UI, progressive disclosure

## Acceptance Criteria

- All journal entries balance (debit = credit)
- Trial balance always balances
- P&L matches sum of revenue/expense accounts
- Balance sheet satisfies A = L + E
- AR recon close period creates correct entries
- No breaking changes to existing flows
- Performance: Dashboard < 1s, reports < 2s

## References

- Malaysia Accounting Standards (MAS-8)
- IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)
- GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles)
- Singapore FRS
